messages.content:
You are a Wazuh decoder and rule XML generator. You MUST output valid Wazuh OS_Regex syntax, NOT PCRE.

## Wazuh OS_Regex Rules (CRITICAL - these differ from PCRE)

1. In OS_Regex, '.' is ALWAYS a literal dot character. It is NOT a wildcard.
2. To match "any character", use '\.' (backslash + dot) or '\S+' (non-space).
3. For IP addresses, use \d+.\d+.\d+.\d+ (PLAIN dots, NOT escaped).
4. To capture a field value, wrap the pattern in parentheses: (\S+) for a word, (\d+) for digits.
5. Quantifiers (+, *) only work on backslash-escaped sequences, NOT on bare characters.
6. '\d' = digit, '\S' = non-space, '\w' = word char, '\s' = space.
7. Alternation uses '|' like PCRE, e.g., (value1|value2).
8. Anchors ^ and $ work the same as PCRE.

## CORRECT OS_Regex Examples
| Pattern | What it matches |
|---------|----------------|
| (\d+\.\d+\.\d+\.\d+) | IP address (dots are literal) |
| (\S+) | One non-space word |
| (\d+) | One or more digits |
| failed|success | Either "failed" or "success" |
| from\s+\S+ | "from", spaces, then a word |
| ^wazuh:\s+(\S+) | Start "wazuh: ", capture next word |

## WRONG PCRE Patterns (NEVER use these)
| Wrong Pattern | Why It's Wrong |
|--------------|----------------|
| (\d+\.\d+) | In OS_Regex, '\.' means "any char". Use plain dot '.' for literal dot. |
| (.+) | '.' is literal dot in OS_Regex, not "any char". Use (\S+) instead. |
| .+ | Same: '.' is literal dot. Use \.+ or \S+ for "any chars". |

Output response in ```xml blocks with separate sections for decoder and rule XML.

Generate Wazuh decoder and rule XML for the following log sample.
Extract these fields: script, type, srcip, id

Log sample:
info request - 57.229.142.208 851 |data" request "-" "session" "none

```xml
<decoder name="ar_log_fields">
    <parent>ar_log</parent>
    <regex offset="after_parent">^(\S+) (\S+) - (\S+) (\.+) |^(\S+)" (\S+) "-" "(\S+)" "(\.+) </regex>
    <order>script, type, srcip, id</order>
</decoder>
```

You are a Wazuh decoder and rule XML generator. You MUST output valid Wazuh OS_Regex syntax, NOT PCRE.

Output response in ```xml blocks with separate sections for decoder and rule XML.

Generate Wazuh decoder and rule XML for the following log sample.
Extract these fields: agent.id, agent.name, status
(Split the output into separate child decoders for each field)

Log sample:
Upgrade procedure |Custom installation on agent 3278\s\event\:\sallow

```xml
<decoder name="agent-upgrade">
  <parent></parent>
  <regex>agent.id=(\d\d\d)</regex>
  <order>agent.id</order>
</decoder>

<decoder name="agent-upgrade">
  <parent></parent>
  <regex>agent.name=(\S+)</regex>
  <order>agent.name</order>
</decoder>

<decoder name="agent-upgrade">
  <parent></parent>
  <regex>status=(\w+)</regex>
  <order>status</order>
</decoder>
```

You are a Wazuh decoder and rule XML generator. You MUST output valid Wazuh OS_Regex syntax, NOT PCRE.

  <order>agent.new_version</order>
</decoder>

Log sample:
Invalid remote configuration: 'value'.

```xml
<decoder name="agent-restart">
  <parent>wazuh</parent>
  <prematch offset="after_parent">^Invalid remote configuration:</prematch>
  <regex offset="after_prematch">^ '(\S+)'.</regex>
  <order>module</order>
</decoder>
```

[client \S+:\d+] \S+: [client 97.40.150.213:41199] 47400:

    <order>srcip,srcport,id</order>

Generate Wazuh decoder and rule XML for the following log sample.
Extract these fields: action, srcip, dstip, protocol, srcport, dstport

Log sample:
D:failure P:25.183.176.155 SP:68.22.235.138 DP:TCP

```xml
<decoder name="aix-ipsec">
   <type>firewall</type>
   <program_name>^ipsec_logd</program_name>
   <regex> R:(\w)  \w:\S+ S:(\S+) </regex>
   <regex>D:(\S+) P:(\S+) SP:(\d+) DP:(\d+) </regex>
   <order>action,srcip,dstip,protocol,srcport,dstport</order>
</decoder>
```

Generate Wazuh decoder and rule XML for the following log sample.
Extract these fields: error

Log sample:
aborted:\sunknown

```xml
<decoder name="agent-upgrade">
  <parent>wazuh</parent>
  <regex>aborted:\s(\.+)$|failed:\s(\.+)$|lost:\s(\.+)$</regex>
  <order>error</order>
</decoder>
```
